# Autogenerated squid with tests

This is a Subsquid indexer (a _squid_) generated by the `create-squid` utility out of the description at `createSquid.yaml`. It captures, decodes and saves to Postgres some smart contract events:
 - from `Tokens` contracts: events
    * `Transfer`
   from the following contract instances:
    * `usdc` on `ethereum-mainnet` (`0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48`)
    * `sqd` on `arbitrum-one` (`0x1337420dED5ADb9980CFc35f8f2B054ea86f8aB1`)
 - from `AavePool` contracts: events
    * `LiquidationCall`
   from the following contract instances:
    * `main` on `ethereum-mainnet` (`0x7d2768dE32b0b80b7a3454c06BdAc94A69DDc7A9`)
Its main source of data is [Subsquid Network](https://docs.sqd.ai/subsquid-network/) that supplies historical data in bulk, allowing for a fast sync. To keep up with network updates [in real time](https://docs.sqd.ai/sdk/resources/unfinalized-blocks/) it uses RPC: public in local runs (see `.env`) or Subsquid Cloud's RPC service when deployed there.

Dependencies: Node.js v20 or newer, Docker.

## Quickstart

Begin by executing the commands that the code generator recommended to you at the end of its run. Once done you can start the indexer with:
```bash
docker compose up -d
sqd run
```
This will start the database, then apply migrations, spawn a processor for each network involved + a GraphQL server. A GraphiQL playground will be available at [localhost:4350/graphql](http://localhost:4350/graphql).

## Testing

Enter the project folder and make sure that the database is up:
```bash
docker compose up -d
```
then run
```bash
npm test
```

## Deploying

```bash
sqd deploy .
```
If this squid uses multiple networks, you're going to need a [professional organization](https://docs.sqd.ai/cloud/resources/organizations/#professional-organizations) to run it.
